description: The Worker-communist Party of Iran — Hekmatist (WPI-H) is a splinter party that emerged following a leadership dispute after the death of [Mansoor Hekmat](/people/mansoor-hekmat) in 2002. The faction led by Koorosh Modarresi formally split from the [Worker-communist Party of Iran](/orgs/worker-communist-party-of-iran) in 2004, claiming to be the legitimate heir to Hekmat's ideological legacy and accusing the Hamid Taqvaee-led WPI of deviating from Hekmat's line. The party operates in exile and publishes theoretical works positioning itself as the orthodox current of worker-communism. The split reflects personal and political disagreements over party direction following Hekmat's death rather than fundamental ideological divergence.
editorial_notes: Koorosh Modarresi-led split from WPI in 2004. Claims Hekmat's legacy. Considered largely defunct by 2010s.
